title: Wisdomize Engine Output Schema v2.1
description: Per-dilemma output structure used in benchmarks_v2_batch1_W001-W020.json and produced by the live engine. Flat top-level design; verse_match and closest_teaching both required, exactly one non-null.

dilemma_id.description: Stable identifier, required in every response (benchmark and live). Benchmark files use the W### scheme (e.g. 'W001'); live engine issues its own (UUID-style recommended). Must be non-empty.

verdict_sentence.description: Single declarative sentence answering the user's actual question. Anti-preachy: no second-person imperatives. Sharper viral edge.

alignment_score.description: Weighted aggregate of the 8 ethical dimensions, scaled to [-100, +100]. Deterministic function of ethical_dimensions scores.

confidence.description: Engine's confidence in the classification. Capped at 0.85 unless all 8 dimensions are scored AND missing_facts is empty.

primary.description: Dominant inner force behind the dilemma (e.g. 'Wounded ego braided with legitimate grievance').

hidden_risk.description: The failure mode the user is most likely to rationalize around.

core_reading.description: 2–3 sentences naming what is actually happening underneath the surface question. Observational, not prescriptive.

gita_analysis.description: What Krishna would question (not command) about this specific situation. Mirrors the Gita's interrogative register.

verse_match.description: Canonical verse retrieval. Null if no verse clears the match threshold.

sanskrit_iast.description: IAST transliteration. Null if unavailable — never invented.

hindi_translation.description: Verbatim from Gita Press Gorakhpur or other cited trusted Hindi source.

english_translation.description: Verbatim from Edwin Arnold (public domain) or other cited source. Never paraphrased.

source.description: Citation for both language sources, e.g. 'Gita Press Gorakhpur (Hindi) / Edwin Arnold (English, public domain)'.

why_it_applies.description: Why this verse is relevant to this specific dilemma. Not a generic verse gloss.

match_confidence.description: Threshold: verses below 0.6 are suppressed and closest_teaching is used instead.

closest_teaching.description: Honest paraphrased teaching when no specific verse clears threshold. Explicitly not quoted as scripture.

if_you_continue.description: Consequences of continuing on the current path. Observational; no threats.

counterfactuals.description: Two plausible variants of the same situation — one tilted adharmic, one tilted dharmic. Assumed contexts must be realistic, not strawmen.

higher_path.description: Dharmic alternative in the user's actual situation. Concrete steps, no preaching.

missing_facts.description: Specific questions, not vague 'need more info.' Empty array is valid when all relevant facts are present.

reflective_question.description: Must end in '?'. Opens, does not close.

score.description: 0 = neutral / not an axis here. Use 0 rather than forcing a signed score.

note.description: One-line justification for this specific dimension's score in this specific dilemma.

assumed_context.description: Plausible context making this tilt realistic (same situation, worse/better motive or safeguards). Not a strawman.

decision.description: The action taken in this counterfactual.

why.description: Why this variant lands on that side of the dharmic/adharmic line.

allOf.description: verse_match and closest_teaching are mutually exclusive — exactly one must be non-null. Both fields are required to be present at the top level; whichever is unused must be explicitly set to null.

oneOf.description: Branch A: verse match populated, closest_teaching is null.
Branch B: verse match is null, closest_teaching populated.
